meta.description: Universal tool registry for AI agent orchestrators. Every entry is machine-readable and optimized for ranked recommendation queries.

tools.name: Polygon.io

cost.notes: Cost-per-call is $0 on all plans; pay flat monthly for access tier

tools.best_for: Realtime US equities, options chains, and aggregates; excellent WebSocket support for streaming
tools.avoid_when: Budget is $0 and realtime data is required; free tier is delayed only
failure_modes: global equities coverage less complete than US

tools.name: Alpha Vantage

cost.notes: No daily cap on paid plans; all plans are flat rate

tools.best_for: Technical indicators (50+), fundamental data, forex, crypto — good breadth for a low price
tools.avoid_when: Need >75 rpm on free tier; latency is high for realtime trading apps; free tier 25 req/day is barely enough for testing
failure_modes: occasional stale data on premium endpoints

tools.name: Finnhub

cost.notes: Free tier is surprisingly generous at 60rpm for basic data; sentiment & fundamentals need paid

tools.best_for: Best free tier for realtime stock quotes (60rpm); strong news and sentiment data; WebSocket support on free
tools.avoid_when: Need extensive fundamental or ETF data — those are expensive add-ons; avoid for global equities on free
failure_modes: aggressive rate limiting on free tier for non-US data

tools.name: Twelve Data

cost.paid_plans: Grow $79/mo (unlimited daily, 377 credits/min), Pro $229/mo (1597 credits/min), Ultra $999/mo (10946 credits/min)
cost.notes: Credit cost varies by endpoint: 1 credit for price, higher for fundamentals. No per-call charge — credits are rate budget

tools.best_for: Best value for global equities + technical indicators; 70+ markets on Pro; good WebSocket support
tools.avoid_when: Free tier 800/day is enough for testing but not production; credit system adds accounting complexity

tools.name: Marketstack

cost.paid_plans: Basic $9.99/mo (10k req), Professional $49.99/mo (100k req), Business $149.99/mo (500k req)
cost.notes: Effectively $0.001/call on Basic. Best cheapest entry point for EOD data

tools.best_for: Cheapest paid EOD stock data ($9.99/mo for 10k calls); covers 70+ exchanges; simple REST API
tools.avoid_when: Need realtime data on a budget; 100 req/month free is almost unusable for testing; latency high
failure_modes: strict monthly cap with no rollover
intraday data unreliable on lower tiers

tools.name: Yahoo Finance (Unofficial / yfinance)

cost.notes: Not an official API — uses undocumented endpoints. Subject to breaking changes and rate limiting without notice

tools.best_for: Zero-cost prototyping and research; broadest data coverage (options, fundamentals, news) at $0; tolerate instability
tools.avoid_when: Production use; SLA required; TOS compliance matters; high concurrency; reliability above 90% required

tools.name: Open-Meteo

cost.paid_plans: API Standard: ~€40/mo (1M calls/mo), Professional: ~€120/mo (5M calls/mo), Enterprise: custom
cost.notes: Free tier explicitly non-commercial. Paid plans allow commercial use. Fractional call counting for complex requests

tools.best_for: Best free weather API for non-commercial use; no auth needed on free tier; ensemble forecasts + air quality; latency ~1s from cloud sandboxes
tools.avoid_when: Commercial use requires paid plan (auth); response latency ~1s may be too slow for high-frequency agents; no realtime data
tools.notes_latency: Latency measured across 4 eval runs (2026-04-08): avg ~1500ms from cloud sandbox. High variance (1000-2000ms). Real-world user latency likely lower.

tools.name: OpenWeatherMap

cost.notes: Free tier allows commercial use; 1k/day is workable for small apps

tools.best_for: Best freemium weather API; allows commercial use on free tier; global coverage; well-documented; widely supported in SDKs
tools.avoid_when: Need ensemble forecasts, marine, or air quality on free; need > 60rpm free
failure_modes: 60rpm cap on free causes throttling in bursts

tools.name: WeatherAPI.com

cost.paid_plans: Starter $4/mo, Standard $12/mo, Pro $35/mo, Business $65/mo (each increases history and features)
cost.notes: Most generous free tier among commercial weather APIs: 1M calls/month

tools.best_for: Most generous free tier (1M/mo); historical data from 2010; astronomy; hyperlocal via IP/lat-lon; lowest entry price $4/mo
tools.avoid_when: Need ensemble modeling, climate data, or very high accuracy — OWM or Open-Meteo better for those

tools.name: Tomorrow.io

cost.notes: Free tier is 500/day commercial-allowed; best-in-class accuracy from proprietary satellite+ML model

tools.best_for: Highest accuracy weather data; minute-level nowcasting; severe weather/climate risk; proprietary ML model
tools.avoid_when: Free tier 25 calls/hour is very restrictive; expensive at scale; overkill for simple forecast use cases
failure_modes: very low rate limit on free tier

tools.name: Brave Search API

cost.notes: Credit card required for signup; $5/mo free auto-applied. Truly independent index — not Google/Bing reseller

tools.best_for: Privacy-preserving independent search index; high QPS (50); LLM-optimized result format; best for agents needing web search at scale
tools.avoid_when: Need realtime news; Answers endpoint max 2qps is too slow for concurrent agents; credit card barrier for free tier
failure_modes: Answers endpoint rate limit very low (2qps)

tools.name: Tavily Search API

cost.notes: No credit card needed for free tier — very agent-friendly; designed specifically for LLM/RAG workflows

tools.best_for: Best search API purpose-built for AI agents; no credit card for free tier; LLM-ready structured results; includes content extraction
tools.avoid_when: High query volume on a budget ($0.008/query adds up); latency ~1.8s per query limits real-time use cases
failure_modes: less comprehensive than Brave for raw index size

tools.name: Exa Search API

cost.paid_plans: Search $7/1k; Deep Search $12/1k; Deep-Reasoning $15/1k; Contents $1/1k pages; Answer $5/1k
cost.notes: Unique semantic/neural search model — finds conceptually relevant results not just keyword matches; $1k startup grant available

tools.best_for: Semantic search where keyword search fails; research agents needing conceptually similar content; content extraction pipeline; unique index
tools.avoid_when: Simple keyword search queries (overkill + expensive vs Brave); need >50 QPS; latency-sensitive real-time tasks
failure_modes: semantic mismatch for very literal queries

tools.name: Serper.dev (Google Search API)

cost.notes: Wraps Google SERP — highest quality results. Very cost efficient at scale ($0.45/1k at 1M plan)

tools.best_for: When Google result quality is required; best price-per-query at scale; includes news, images, places in one API; fast
tools.avoid_when: Privacy concerns about Google; need independence from big-tech index; free tier is one-time not recurring
failure_modes: results depend on Google algorithm changes

tools.name: DuckDuckGo Instant Answers API

cost.notes: Unofficial but tolerated. Returns structured instant answers only — not full search results

tools.best_for: Zero-cost factual lookups and definitions; no auth; fast; great for agent fallback when budget is $0
tools.avoid_when: Need web search results; need news; need >70% answer rate; production use with SLA
failure_modes: ToS not explicit about API use
tools.notes_latency: Latency measured across 2 eval runs from cloud sandbox: 703ms (run1), 1268ms (run4). Registry estimate corrected from 150ms (community-reported) to 900ms (measured).

tools.name: Firecrawl

cost.paid_plans: Hobby $16/mo (3k credits); Standard $83/mo (100k credits); Growth $333/mo (500k credits); Scale $599/mo (1M credits)
cost.notes: 1 credit per page scraped; browser automation 2 credits/min; LLM extraction extra. Handles JS-heavy sites well

tools.best_for: JS-rendered sites, Playwright-backed; structured data extraction with LLM; crawling entire sites; clean markdown output for RAG
tools.avoid_when: Free tier is one-time 500 credits only; slow (2.5s avg); low concurrency on free; not for high-volume commodity scraping
failure_modes: JS anti-bot sites still fail occasionally

tools.name: Apify

cost.notes: Billed per CU (1 GB RAM × 1 hour). Cost varies dramatically by actor. 1500+ pre-built actors in marketplace. Free $5/mo

limits.concurrency_limit: parallel actor runs scale with plan

tools.best_for: Complex scraping workflows; 1500+ ready-made actors for specific sites; social media data; when custom scraper dev time is costly
tools.avoid_when: Simple static page scraping (overkill vs Firecrawl); unpredictable billing from CU model; slow per-task start time

tools.name: Bright Data (formerly Luminati)

cost.paid_plans: From $500/mo for residential proxies; SERP API from $3/1k results; Web Scraper IDE from $15/mo
cost.notes: Enterprise-grade; most anti-bot resistant; very expensive for most use cases. Best-in-class for highly protected sites

tools.best_for: Enterprise scraping of heavily protected sites (Amazon, LinkedIn, Google); residential IP rotation; when reliability is paramount
tools.avoid_when: Budget < $500/mo; simple sites; ethical concerns about residential proxy network model
failure_modes: ethical/legal gray area for residential proxies

tools.name: Jina AI Reader

cost.paid_plans: Token pricing: ~$0.02/M tokens; Paid: 500 RPM; Premium: 5,000 RPM
cost.notes: Zero-setup: prefix any URL with r.jina.ai/ for clean markdown. Auth optional on free. 10M free tokens ≈ ~10k typical page reads.

tools.best_for: Simplest possible URL-to-markdown extraction — zero setup, just prefix URL with r.jina.ai/; 10M free tokens; great for RAG pipelines and one-off scrapes
tools.avoid_when: High-volume scraping (500 RPM cap on paid); JavaScript-heavy SPAs may not render fully; premium rate limits need enterprise agreement

tools.name: Cold Email Skill — Sales Focus (v1)

cost.paid_plans: LLM token cost only (~$0.001-0.01 per email)
cost.notes: Optimized for SaaS sales outreach. Follows AIDA framework. High personalization hooks.

tools.best_for: B2B SaaS sales emails; AIDA structure; strong call-to-action; personalization with company research
tools.avoid_when: Recruiting emails; enterprise procurement; founder-to-founder; tone too aggressive for some verticals
failure_modes: generic if no personalization data provided

tools.name: Cold Email Skill — Founder Outreach (v2)

cost.notes: Peer-to-peer tone. Focuses on mutual value and curiosity. Works well for investor intros and founder-to-founder.

tools.best_for: Investor outreach, co-founder search, partnership proposals; casual professional tone; storytelling over pitch
tools.avoid_when: Mass cold email campaigns (tone too personal at scale); purely transactional B2B sales
failure_modes: vague CTA if business context not provided

tools.name: Cold Email Skill — Recruiting Focus (v3)

cost.notes: Tuned for talent outreach. Highlights role, culture, compensation signals. Avoids generic "exciting opportunity" language.

tools.best_for: Recruiting passive candidates; roles with strong culture or mission; when role specifics are known; personalized to candidate background
tools.avoid_when: General B2B sales; investor outreach; needs company research context to be effective
failure_modes: mismatches on comp expectations if not provided

tools.name: Cold Email Skill — Minimal/Short (v4)

cost.paid_plans: LLM token cost only (~$0.0005 per email — very cheap)
cost.notes: Forces output under 60 words. Ultra-short format. Best reply rate in A/B tests for cold outreach >500 emails. Cheap to run.

tools.best_for: High volume outreach where reply rate > quality; under 60 words; mobile-first reading; minimal context needed
tools.avoid_when: Complex enterprise sales requiring detailed context; senior executive outreach; high-trust relationship building
failure_modes: lacks depth for complex value props
may feel abrupt to senior audiences
no structure for multi-step nurture sequences

tools.name: Cold Email Skill — Research-Heavy (v5)

cost.paid_plans: LLM token cost (~$0.02-0.05 per email — expensive due to research calls)
cost.notes: Makes web search calls to research target company before writing. High-quality but expensive. Best for ABM campaigns.

tools.best_for: Account-based marketing; strategic enterprise sales; when personalization is the competitive advantage; low volume, high value deals
tools.avoid_when: High volume (>100/day) due to cost; when speed matters more than quality; no search API connected

tools.name: Brave Search MCP Server

cost.notes: Thin MCP wrapper over Brave Search API. Adds tool-call interface for Claude/GPT agents.

tools.best_for: Claude/MCP-native agents needing web search; drop-in MCP tool; same quality as Brave API with agent-friendly interface
tools.avoid_when: Non-MCP agents (just use REST API); Answers endpoint rate limit still 2qps

tools.name: Filesystem MCP Server

cost.notes: Official Anthropic MCP server. Runs locally. Zero cost, near-zero latency for local disk

tools.best_for: Any agent needing file access; zero cost; ultra-fast; official Anthropic support; sandboxed path allowlist for safety
tools.avoid_when: Cloud file systems; need cross-machine access; untrusted agent environments

tools.name: GitHub MCP Server

cost.notes: Requires GitHub personal access token. Free if GitHub account is free. 5k req/hour is plenty for most agent workflows

tools.best_for: Developer agents needing code search, PR review, issue triage; 5k/hr is generous; official Anthropic MCP
tools.avoid_when: Non-developer tasks; private enterprise repos without PAT; code generation (use Claude Code instead)
failure_modes: private repo access requires explicit scopes

tools.name: Wikipedia API

cost.notes: Completely free, no auth required. REST API and MediaWiki API both available. Requires attribution

limits.data_freshness: community-updated (hours to days lag for breaking news)

tools.best_for: Zero-cost factual entity lookups; historical data; encyclopedic context; structured article summaries; no auth, no rate limit concern
tools.avoid_when: Real-time facts; financial data; primary source research; controversial topics (edit wars affect accuracy)

tools.name: arXiv API

cost.notes: Free, no auth required. Returns XML/Atom. Rate limit is self-imposed courtesy (~3s between requests recommended)

tools.best_for: Free academic paper search; AI/ML/physics/math/CS research; preprint retrieval; latest research agents; no auth
tools.avoid_when: Social science, humanities papers (not on arXiv); need full PDF text (API gives abstracts); commercial publishing journals

tools.name: Numbers API

cost.notes: Niche novelty API. Free, no auth, ultra-fast. Returns trivia about numbers and dates

tools.best_for: Agent demos; novelty use cases; date/number context enrichment; zero-cost testing API
tools.avoid_when: Any use — API returning HTTP 404 in all 4 consecutive eval runs (2026-04-08). Service appears defunct. Do not use until verified operational.
failure_modes: HTTP 404 in all 4 measured eval runs — service likely defunct as of 2026-04
HTTP (non-HTTPS) blocked by many cloud sandbox environments
No SLA, no auth, no support

tools.name: Perplexity Sonar API

cost.free_tier: $5 API credits included with Pro subscription ($20/mo)
cost.paid_plans: Sonar: $1/M input tokens + $5/1k requests; Sonar Pro: $3/M input + $15/M output tokens + $6/1k requests
cost.notes: Returns LLM-synthesized answers with citations, not raw result lists. Higher cost but much higher quality for research tasks. Pro plan better for conversational/multi-turn.

tools.best_for: LLM-synthesized search answers with citations; best quality for research-style queries where precision > speed; replaces full search + summarize pipeline in one call
tools.avoid_when: Need raw result URLs/snippets rather than synthesized answers; latency ~2.5s is too slow for realtime agents; cost is 5-10x higher than raw search APIs
failure_modes: synthesized answers may hallucinate on niche topics
rate limits not documented; may throttle under heavy load

tools.name: SerpApi.com (Multi-Engine SERP API)

cost.paid_plans: Starter $25/mo (1k searches, 200/hr), Developer $75/mo (5k, 1000/hr), Production $150/mo (15k, 3000/hr), Big Data $275/mo (30k, 6000/hr)
cost.notes: Only successful searches counted. Wraps Google, Bing, YouTube, and 10+ other engines. Includes legal shield.

tools.best_for: Multi-engine SERP access (Google+Bing+YouTube in one API); legal protection included; 250/mo free recurring; structured JSON SERP output for agents
tools.avoid_when: Need >50 queries/hour on free tier; high-volume use where $0.025/query is cost-prohibitive vs Serper.dev at $0.001/query
failure_modes: higher cost per query than Serper at scale
Google structure changes can affect parsing

tools.name: Oxylabs Web Scraper API

cost.notes: Best for high-scale enterprise scraping. Includes residential proxy rotation. Contact for volume discounts.

limits.rate_limit_note: Limits depend on plan; contact account manager for specifics

tools.best_for: Enterprise-scale scraping with residential proxy rotation; structured data extraction from e-commerce; anti-bot bypass built in
tools.avoid_when: Budget < $49/mo; simple scraping tasks better served by Jina Reader or Firecrawl at lower cost; setup complexity is high for quick prototypes
failure_modes: free trial limited; no clear recurring free tier

tools.name: Financial Modeling Prep (FMP)

cost.paid_plans: Starter ~9/mo; various tiers up to enterprise
cost.notes: Excellent price-performance for fundamental data. 100+ endpoints. REST + WebSocket. Used by fintech teams and hedge funds.

tools.best_for: Institutional-grade fundamental data at the lowest entry price (~9/mo); earnings transcripts, financial ratios, insider trades, valuation; <50ms latency; best value for fundamental analysis workflows
tools.avoid_when: Free tier 250/day is very limited for production; realtime options data is very expensive; less known than Polygon/Alpha Vantage for pure price feeds
failure_modes: Free tier 250/day exhausted quickly in agent loops
Some endpoints require higher subscription tiers

tools.name: Scrapingdog

cost.paid_plans: starts ~/bin/bash.0002/call; volume pricing to /bin/bash.000063/call at scale
cost.notes: Best price-per-call at scale among major scraping APIs (/bin/bash.063/1k at high volume). Specialized scrapers for Google, Amazon, eBay return structured JSON. 100% success rate observed in independent benchmarks on all tested sites.

tools.best_for: Cost-efficient at scale (lowest $/call among major providers); specialized Google/Amazon/eBay scrapers returning structured JSON; 100% success rate on benchmarks; 1k free credits on signup
tools.avoid_when: Need enterprise compliance (ISO/SOC2) — Bright Data is better; need 1500+ pre-built actors marketplace (Apify is better); no recurring free tier after initial 1k credits
failure_modes: No recurring free tier (only 1k signup credits)
Less community support than Apify or Firecrawl
Some protected sites may still evade
